govukComponents.usage: Groups the address block under legend 'Consignee'
Field labels; 'Consignee name' uses govuk-label--m, address sub-fields use plain govuk-label
Consignee name, address lines 1-3, city/town, postcode, telephone, email
'Return to search' link back to consignee search

fields.label: Consignee name

Note: tests always populate it (DEFAULT_CONFIG consigneeAddress.addressLine2='Suite 3'), so no trace exercises the empty-optional path

fields.name: city-or-town
fields.label: City or town

fields.name: postcode
fields.label: Postcode or ZIP code
fields.evidence: trace db2d277c5c8bfcf2dc0f2278bc92dc7aa8fdf42d action 104 (id/name=postcode). required unknown from DOM — not marked optional but no HTML required attr; marked false pending validation evidence. Page object: ConsigneeCreationPage.ts:27-29 (inputPostcodeOrZipCode 'Postcode or ZIP code'). observedValue '10001' is DIRECTLY OBSERVED in this trace at action 105 (Fill '10001' into the postcode field, contiguous with the action-104 city fill in the consignee block, actions 100-108) — it also matches DEFAULT_CONFIG consigneeAddress.postcode (ched-p-workflows.ts:169), so it is confirmed reality, not merely config-inferred

fields.name: telephone
fields.label: Telephone number
fields.evidence: trace db2d277c5c8bfcf2dc0f2278bc92dc7aa8fdf42d action 104 (id/name=telephone, input type=tel — control is a tel input, not a plain text input). Page object: ConsigneeCreationPage.ts:31-33 (inputTelephoneNumber 'Telephone number'). observedValue '01100100100' is DIRECTLY OBSERVED in this trace at action 106 (Fill '01100100100' into the telephone field); also matches DEFAULT_CONFIG consigneeAddress.telephone — ched-p-workflows.ts:170

fields.name: country
fields.label: Country
fields.evidence: trace db2d277c5c8bfcf2dc0f2278bc92dc7aa8fdf42d action 104 (id/name=country). 254 options incl. placeholder 'Please select your country'. Full country reference list — first 20 verbatim; new app should source this from a reference-data service, not hardcode. Note: list splits 'United Kingdom' into England/Scotland/Wales/Northern Ireland as separate options. Page object: ConsigneeCreationPage.ts:35-37 (dropdownCountry getByLabel 'Country'), selected by OPTION VALUE not visible text — fillAddress calls selectOption(address.country) where Address.country holds a code (types/country.ts:238-241: England='GB-ENG', NorthernIreland='GB-NIR', Scotland='GB-SCT', Wales='GB-WLS'). observedValue 'England' (value GB-ENG) is DIRECTLY OBSERVED in this trace at action 107 (Select option -> return value ['GB-ENG']); also matches DEFAULT_CONFIG consigneeAddress.country=country.England — ched-p-workflows.ts:171. So the <select> option value is a country CODE and the option text is the country NAME. required=true is INFERRED from the placeholder option 'Please select your country' (conventionally a required select) — not DOM-enforced (no HTML required attr; req=false), consistent with the required-ness gap noted in openQuestions

fields.name: email
fields.label: Email address
fields.evidence: trace db2d277c5c8bfcf2dc0f2278bc92dc7aa8fdf42d action 104 (id/name=email, input type=email — control is an email input, not a plain text input). Page object: ConsigneeCreationPage.ts:39-41 (inputEmailAddress 'Email address'). observedValue 'globalcorp@mail.com' is DIRECTLY OBSERVED in this trace at action 108 (Fill 'globalcorp@mail.com' into the email field); also matches DEFAULT_CONFIG consigneeAddress.email — ched-p-workflows.ts:172

corroboration.workflow: workflows/notification/ched-p-workflows.ts:406-408 (create-new-consignee path) and 752-756 (DEFAULT_CONFIG path); ched-d-workflows.ts:272-274 uses the identical page object and Address type — the consignee creation form is generic across CHED types
corroboration.domainType: types/address.ts (Address: name, addressLine1, addressLine2, addressLine3, cityOrTown, postcode, telephone, country, email)
corroboration.summary: The page object exposes EXACTLY the 9 fields already in the spec and no others — no variant-only control was missed. All 9 are always populated by fillAddress(), so no trace or test exercises an empty-field or missing-required path. No test in tests/notification/ched-p/ or the accessibility specs asserts any validation message, status or rule about this page (the accessibility spec at tests/accessibility/ched-p-accessibility-tests.spec.ts:159-171 only axe-checks 'Consignee Creation Page' after a full fill). Validation copy therefore remains entirely unmined — an honest gap, not an absence of requirement.

openQuestions: Which fields are truly required? No field carries an HTML 'required' attribute — IPAFFS validates server-side. 'Consignee name' and 'Address line 1' appear mandatory (no '(optional)' suffix); lines 2/3 are explicitly optional. Postcode/telephone/country/email required-ness could not be confirmed from the DOM, and the QA tests always fill every field (fillAddress populates all 9), so no test asserts required-ness either. No validation-error trace was captured on this page. GAP.

This trace is CHED-P Fish (the IPAFFS route the IUU journey is carved from). The consignee creation form is generic across CHED types — the same ConsigneeCreationPage page object and Address type drive CHED-P and CHED-D (ched-d-workflows.ts:272-274). Confirm the IUU rebuild needs the same consignee capture step at journey position 26.

The country <select> lists 254 options and splits the UK into England/Scotland/Wales/Northern Ireland as distinct options; option VALUES are country codes (GB-ENG/GB-NIR/GB-SCT/GB-WLS, etc. — types/country.ts:238-241) while option TEXT is the country name. New app should confirm the canonical country reference set and code scheme from a reference-data service, and decide whether the UK-split is retained.

No hint text and no error summary rendered on this snapshot (happy-path, mid-form fill), and no test asserts any validation message for this page. Validation copy for the consignee form remains entirely unmined — no error-state trace and no test-level expectation exist. GAP; a question for a human.

The consignee creation form is reached only via the 'create new consignee' branch (searchExistingConsignee.linkCreateNewConsignee) after an address-book search misses — ched-p-workflows.ts:403-407. The IUU rebuild should confirm whether it retains the search-existing-then-create address-book pattern or captures the consignee inline.
